frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: perceptual choices arise from build-up of action-selective signals over time

frameworks.name: dynamic prior expectations
frameworks.one_line: history biases instantiate trial-by-trial priors adapted to environmental statistics

frameworks.name: behavioral modeling of history bias
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial choice/outcome history weights estimated from behavior

core_question: Does the environment's correlation structure adaptively shape how history biases enter motor cortical decision build-up?
key_assumptions: history biases act on the accumulation of evidence rather than on early sensory encoding
action-selective motor cortical activity indexes decision formation
observers can adapt bias weights to the statistical structure of the environment
manipulations: correlation structure of the sensory environment across blocks
trial-by-trial history of prior choices and outcomes
key_findings: behavioral history biases flexibly tracked the environment's correlation structure
history bias modulated the build-up rate of action-selective motor cortical activity
baseline level of motor cortical activity was not affected by history bias
history-bias effect added to the impact of the current stimulus on build-up
interpretations: motor cortical build-up reflects dynamic prior expectations from adaptive environmental interaction
action plans are shaped by an additive combination of current evidence and history-derived priors
